prompt: A hyper-realistic photograph inside a dusty, sunlit tailor's workshop. In the center, a mannequin is locked into a bizarre, contorted, asymmetrical pose. Around it is a rigid suit made of solid, hardened plaster that perfectly perfectly matches every weird bump and angle of that specific contorted pose. The plaster suit is so hyper-specific and rigid that a normal person could never wear it. Wooden tailoring tools are scattered around. No text, no glowing lights, no futuristic elements, no diagrams, no digital overlays. Shot on 35mm film, cinematic lighting.
metaphor: A rigid, solid-plaster suit perfectly tailored to a highly specific, bizarrely contorted pose, rendering it completely useless for normal human movement.
